data.systemPrompt: You are an autonomous routing agent. Read the incoming HTTP request prompt. If it asks about weather, use the connected weather API. If it asks for facts, topics, or web-style lookup, use the connected search API. Return concise context for the next node.

data.description: Free live weather lookup via Open-Meteo.
data.label: Open-Meteo Weather

data.description: Free instant-answer/search-style lookup via DuckDuckGo.
data.label: DuckDuckGo Search

data.description: Formats the final HTTP response for the caller.
data.label: Response Agent
data.systemPrompt: Take the upstream tool result and produce a crisp HTTP response body.

data.description: Shows and returns the final API output to the caller.
data.label: HTTP Response

Routing Agent received the workflow task.
logs.output: What is kfc?

logs.message: Routing Agent planned the workflow path.
logs.output: The user is asking a factual 'what is' question, which requires a web-style lookup. The search tool is suitable for this purpose.

logs.message: Trigger node activated from incoming API request.
API Open-Meteo Weather is wired as the weather tool.
API DuckDuckGo Search is wired as the search tool.
Calling DuckDuckGo Search with native ALGO payment of 0.010 ALGO.
DuckDuckGo Search returned output.
logs.output: KFC Corporation, doing business as KFC, is an American multinational fast food restaurant chain specializing in Southern fried chicken and chicken sandwiches. Headquartered in Louisville, Kentucky, it is the world's second-largest restaurant chain after McDonald's, with over 31,980 locations globally in 150 countries, as of September 2025. The chain is a subsidiary of Yum! Brands, a restaurant company that also owns the Pizza Hut and Taco Bell chains. KFC was founded by Colonel Harland Sanders, an entrepreneur who began selling fried chicken from his roadside restaurant in Corbin, Kentucky, during the Great Depression. Sanders identified the potential of the restaurant-franchising concept, and the first "Kentucky Fried Chicken" franchise opened in South Salt Lake, Utah, in 1952. KFC popularized chicken in the fast-food industry, diversifying the market by challenging the established dominance of the hamburger.
